name: Sugarcane
description: Sugarcane (Saccharum spp.) is a major tropical industrial crop grown for sugar, molasses, and bioethanol.

diseases.name: Red Rot
diseases.description: A highly destructive stalk rot disease that causes internal reddening and major yield loss.
symptoms: Internal stalk tissue turns red with white patches
Leaves yellow and dry from tip downward
Infected stalks may emit sour alcoholic smell
treatments: Treat setts with Carbendazim (0.1%) or hot water

diseases.name: Smut
diseases.description: A systemic disease producing black whip-like structures and severe stunting.
symptoms: Black whip emerges from shoot tip
Whip surface is covered with black powdery spores
Plants become stunted and unproductive
treatments: Treat setts with hot water or Carbendazim
Burn smutted whips before spore release

diseases.name: Wilt
diseases.description: A vascular and stalk disease causing tissue shredding, wilting, and reduced cane weight.
symptoms: Internal tissues show shredding and pink-red discoloration
Leaves yellow and wilt progressively
Stalks become hollow and lightweight

diseases.name: Pokkah Boeng
diseases.description: A leaf and top rot disease causing distortion, chlorosis, and collapse of growing points.
symptoms: Young leaves are twisted and crumpled
Irregular chlorotic patches on leaves
Top rot and stalk tip collapse in severe cases
treatments: Maintain balanced potassium and zinc nutrition

diseases.name: Leaf Scald
diseases.description: A systemic vascular disease causing white leaf streaks, stunting, and sudden wilting in severe infections.
symptoms: Pencil-thin white or pale yellow leaf stripes
Chronic chlorosis with severe stunting
Acute shoot wilting and dieback

diseases.name: Brown Spot
diseases.description: A foliar spot disease reducing photosynthetic area and cane vigor under humid conditions.
symptoms: Small yellowish spots enlarge into brown oval lesions
Lesions may have yellow halos
Coalescing lesions reduce effective leaf area
treatments: Spray Mancozeb 75 WP (2.5 g/L) or Zineb (0.2%)
Improve drainage and reduce excess nitrogen
Repeat sprays at about 14-day intervals if needed

diseases.name: Pineapple Disease
diseases.description: A sett rot disease causing fruity odor, tissue blackening, and poor germination.
symptoms: Cut setts develop pineapple-like smell
Internal tissue turns red then black
Low germination and pre-emergence rot
treatments: Treat cut setts with Carbendazim or Thiram

diseases.name: Ring Spot
diseases.description: A leaf spot disease with ring-like lesions that can dry foliage and reduce sucrose yield.
symptoms: Circular to oval dark brown spots
Reddish-purple border with straw-colored center
Premature leaf drying under heavy infection
treatments: Spray Mancozeb (0.25%) or Copper oxychloride (3 g/L)

diseases.name: Sugarcane Mosaic
diseases.pathogen: Sugarcane mosaic virus; vectors: aphids (Melanaphis sacchari, Rhopalosiphum maidis)
diseases.description: A viral disease causing leaf mosaic, stunting, and reduced sugar recovery.
symptoms: Pale green and yellow mosaic streaks on young leaves
Leaves may become shorter and narrower
Stunted growth and lower sucrose yield

diseases.name: Grassy Shoot Disease
diseases.pathogen: Sugarcane grassy shoot phytoplasma; vector: Pyrilla perpusilla
diseases.description: A phytoplasma disease with excessive grassy tillers and complete loss of usable cane.
symptoms: Many thin pale yellow shoots giving grassy appearance
Leaves become narrow and chlorotic
No normal millable stalk production

diseases.name: Root Knot
diseases.description: A nematode disease that causes root galling, stunting, and decline in cane growth and sugar content.
symptoms: Stunted and yellowing plants
Root gall or knot formation
Reduced tillering and weak root system
treatments: Apply approved nematicides at planting as per label and regulations
